div1.entryFree: Crouch, to lie close to the ground, to stoop, to cringe: to c. in litter of your stable planks, John V, 2, 140. when --ing marrow in the bearer strong cries of itself 'No more,' Tim. V, 4, 9. must I stand and c. under your testy humour? Caes. IV, 3, 45. Followed by for == to fawn in order to obtain sth.: at his heels, leashed in like hounds, should famine, sword and fire c. for employment, Caes. IV, 3, 45.
